value.rationale: The claim is about a specific "Codex guardrails" safety post (allegedly dated May 8, 2026) that frames coding-agent adoption as a control system covering sandboxing, approvals, network policy, credentials, and telemetry. The fetched page is the Codex developer landing/navigation page. While the navigation contains items like "Sandboxing," "Agent approvals & security," "Codex Security," "Permissions," and a general "Guardrails" link (under Agents SDK), there is no mention of a "Codex guardrails" safety post, no May 8, 2026 post, and no body content describing the asserted enterprise control framework. The page body only describes Codex as a coding agent for writing, reviewing, and debugging code. The specific offering described in the claim is not substantiated.
